book_claims.claim: Consuming vegetables at each meal prevents chronic disease and maximises health
book_claims.mechanism: Provides unique mixture of vegetable fibres, vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals which after gut microbe processing release metabolites that protect from common diseases

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis of 95 studies (142 publications): higher vegetable intake associated with reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, cancer, and all-cause mortality. Estimated 7.8 million premature deaths worldwide attributable to insufficient fruit and vegetable intake.

Dietary fiber from vegetables promotes beneficial gut microbiota diversity and short-chain fatty acid production. The fiber-microbiome axis is now recognized as a key mechanism for vegetable health benefits.

Each daily portion of vegetables associated with 4% reduction in all-cause mortality in a meta-analysis of 16 prospective studies. The dose-response plateaued at approximately 5 servings/day for all-cause mortality.

taste: varied: sweet, bitter, earthy, peppery, mild
texture: crunchy to tender depending on type and preparation

culinary_pairings.source: Universal fat carrier for fat-soluble nutrients in vegetables
Foundational aromatic in virtually every cuisine that cooks vegetables
Book recommends as complementary fiber-rich foods; combined fiber supports microbiome diversity
Part of the recommended fiber-rich food group alongside vegetables, beans, nuts, seeds
Zero-calorie flavor enhancement that encourages higher vegetable consumption
Acid enhances flavor and mineral absorption from vegetables
Part of the book's recommended daily dietary pattern alongside vegetables

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Different vegetables provide different fiber types (cellulose, pectin, inulin). Diversity of vegetable fiber supports microbiome diversity.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Thousands of unique compounds across vegetable families
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Many phytochemicals are metabolized by gut bacteria into bioactive metabolites. The combination from diverse vegetables provides broader protection than any single vegetable.
Highly bioavailable from food sources. Most adults do not meet adequate intake (4700 mg/day). Vegetables are a primary source.
Dark green vegetables are the richest sources. Essential for DNA methylation and cell division.

synergies.description: Part of the recommended fibre-rich food group alongside beans, whole grains, nuts, seeds and fruit
